title: Before vs After
subtitle: See exactly what changes when you apply Builder Pattern

scenarios.title: Object Creation -- Telescoping vs Builder
scenarios.description: 15 constructor parameters with 9 false values vs clean fluent Builder API.

before.label: Telescoping Constructor (unreadable, fragile)

after.label: Builder -- Self-Documenting, Extensible

scenarios.insight: Every parameter is named. Optional fields are simply omitted. New fields don't break existing code.

scenarios.title: Object Safety -- Raw Setters vs Validated Builder
scenarios.description: Raw setters allow incomplete objects. Builder ensures objects are born valid and complete.

before.label: Mutable Setters (unsafe, no completion guarantee)

after.label: Builder -- Validated at Birth

scenarios.insight: Builder: messy drafts BEFORE build(), validated object AFTER. Setters: no completion guarantee. Builder gives you a clear 'born valid' moment.

scenarios.title: Validation -- Scattered vs Centralized in build()
scenarios.description: Cross-field validation scattered across setters vs centralized in one build() method.

before.label: Scattered Validation (incomplete, order-dependent)

after.label: Centralized in build() -- Complete Picture

scenarios.insight: build() sees ALL fields at once. Cross-field validation is trivial. No order dependency. No incomplete checks.
